commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ed67ab4ea7b8e9c555685eb030d74f96c63525ff)
commands.review_summary:
This PR optimizes task registry listing and maintenance sweeps, adds `tasks.retentionMs` and `tasks.sweepIntervalMs` config/schema/docs/types, and wires gateway plus manual maintenance to the resolved task config.

PR surface: Source +120, Tests +151, Docs +22. Total +293 across 16 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows the PR changes childless task backing checks to always return backed, while current-main tests require childless Codex-native mirrors to remain live for 30 minutes and then reconcile to `lost`; I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: This PR needs author or maintainer follow-up plus refreshed current-head proof; ClawSweeper should not auto-repair because real behavior proof remains a contributor merge gate.

target.title: perf(tasks): index-driven sweep + configurable retention/interval
target.body:
## What

`runTaskRegistryMaintenance` is the dominant ~minute-cadence offender on busy gateways post-qmd-export-fix (~10% combined self-time across `listTaskRecords` / `cloneTaskRecord`). On Eddie's stack: 1,414 retained terminal tasks, all stamped with `cleanup_after`, none past-due — the sweep walked and re-cloned every one of them every 60s for nothing.

This PR fixes the inefficiencies and adds two long-missing config knobs.

## Why

Two compounding issues + two unconfigurable constants:

1. **`listTaskRecords` did three full passes** (clone → sort → re-clone-to-strip an `insertionIndex` that was redundant with Map iteration order). Called once per sweep AND once per terminal-ACP task inside the sweep loop → O(N²) cloning worst case.
2. **No fast-skip** for the common case (terminal task, `cleanup_after` stamped, not yet due, non-ACP runtime). Every record fully re-iterated and re-cloned with nothing to do.
3. `TASK_RETENTION_MS = 7 days` hardcoded — no config knob.
4. `TASK_SWEEP_INTERVAL_MS = 60_000` hardcoded.

## Changes

- **`listTaskRecords`**: single-pass clone + stable sort. Reverse the input once, then sort on `createdAt` only — Map insertion order + stable sort gives the same "newest first, latest insert breaks ties" semantic without the `insertionIndex` round-trip. Same for `listTasksFromIndex`.
- **`runTaskRegistryMaintenance`**: only invoke `cleanupTerminalAcpSession` for `runtime === 'acp'` (no-op otherwise on >99% of records); when invoked, reuse the top-of-sweep snapshot instead of re-listing per task. Same fix for `cleanupOrphanedParentOwnedAcpSessions`.
- **Fast-skip path**: terminal + stamped + not-due + non-ACP records skip the `getTaskById` clone entirely.
- **New config**: `tasks.retentionMs` (default 7d) and `tasks.sweepIntervalMs` (default 60s) via a small new `task-registry.runtime-config.ts`. Resolved and applied in `startTaskRegistryMaintenance(cfg)`. Existing stamped records keep their original `cleanupAfter`; new terminal records use the configured value. Invalid/non-positive/non-finite values fall back to defaults.

## Validation

- All 292 task-registry tests pass.
- New tests for the runtime-config module (6) and the zod schema for `tasks.*` (8). Both green.
- `pnpm config:schema:gen` produces stable output (`--check` passes).
- `tsgo:core`, `tsgo:core:test` clean.
- `oxlint` clean (`toReversed` instead of `reverse`).

## NON-goals (filed separately)

- The empty-dirty-set `indexAll` fence in `manager-session-sync-state.ts`.
- `runPinnedWriteHelper`'s python3 spawn-per-write architecture.
- Per-status retention (succeeded vs failed retention separation).

---

🚧 **Draft for review** — opening to walk the diff together before marking ready.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Task registry index-driven listing/maintenance and `tasks.sweepIntervalMs` config bounds from PR #77395.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw topic branch `perf/task-registry-index-sweep` at `5e02fdc0d4`, isolated temporary state directory, production task registry/CLI code paths.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran the topic-branch OpenClaw CLI and a production-code driver that populated 5,000 task records, exercised `listTaskRecords()` and `runTaskRegistryMaintenance()`, and checked config-load behavior for `tasks.sweepIntervalMs`.
- **Evidence after fix**: Full copied terminal output is in the proof comment: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/77395#issuecomment-4384871055

  Excerpt of copied live output:
  ```text
  OpenClaw 2026.5.5 (5e02fdc)
  INVALID_CONFIG for tasks.sweepIntervalMs=2592000000
  accepted tasks.sweepIntervalMs=2147483647
  populated task records: 5000
  listTaskRecords cold: ~1 ms
  listTaskRecords warm: ~0.96 ms
  runTaskRegistryMaintenance pruned: 2500
  fast-skip re-sweep over active records: ~1.92 ms
  cleanupAfter - endedAt = 3600000 ms
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: The Node timer maximum is enforced at config load, valid maximum is accepted, task listing keeps newest-first ordering, maintenance prunes due terminal tasks, fast-skip avoids expensive work on active records, and configured retention is honored instead of the default.
- **What was not tested**: Nothing else for this PR's changed behavior beyond the isolated local runtime/CLI proof and supplemental automated validation.
target.author: zeroaltitude

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

- **`runTaskRegistryMaintenance`**: only invoke `cleanupTerminalAcpSession` for `runtime === 'acp'` (no-op otherwise on >99% of records); when invoked, reuse the top-of-sweep snapshot instead of re-listing per task. Same fix for `cleanupOrphanedParentOwnedAcpSessions`.
- **Fast-skip path**: terminal + stamped + not-due + non-ACP records skip the `getTaskById` clone entirely.
- **New config**: `tasks.retentionMs` (default 7d) and `tasks.sweepIntervalMs` (default 60s) via a small new `task-registry.runtime-config.ts`. Resolved and applied in `startTaskRegistryMaintenance(cfg)`. Existing stamped records keep their original `cleanupAfter`; new terminal records use the configured value. Invalid/non-positive/non-finite values fall back to defaults.

## Validation

- All 292 task-registry tests pass.
- New tests for the runtime-config module (6) and the zod schema for `tasks.*` (8). Both green.
- `pnpm config:schema:gen` produces stable output (`--check` passes).
- `tsgo:core`, `tsgo:core:test` clean.
- `oxlint` clean (`toReversed` instead of `reverse`).

## NON-goals (filed separately)

- The empty-dirty-set `indexAll` fence in `manager-session-sync-state.ts`.
- `runPinnedWriteHelper`'s python3 spawn-per-write architecture.
- Per-status retention (succeeded vs failed retention separation).

---

🚧 **Draft for review** — opening to walk the diff together before marking ready.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Task registry index-driven listing/maintenance and `tasks.sweepIntervalMs` config bounds from PR #77395.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw topic branch `perf/task-registry-index-sweep` at `5e02fdc0d4`, isolated temporary state directory, production task registry/CLI code paths.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran the topic-branch OpenClaw CLI and a production-code driver that populated 5,000 task records, exercised `listTaskRecords()` and `runTaskRegistryMaintenance()`, and checked config-load behavior for `tasks.sweepIntervalMs`.
- **Evidence after fix**: Full copied terminal output is in the proof comment: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/77395#issuecomment-4384871055

  Excerpt of copied live output:
  ```text
  OpenClaw 2026.5.5 (5e02fdc)
  INVALID_CONFIG for tasks.sweepIntervalMs=2592000000
  accepted tasks.sweepIntervalMs=2147483647
  populated task records: 5000
  listTaskRecords cold: ~1 ms
  listTaskRecords warm: ~0.96 ms
  runTaskRegistryMaintenance pruned: 2500
  fast-skip re-sweep over active records: ~1.92 ms
  cleanupAfter - endedAt = 3600000 ms
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: The Node timer maximum is enforced at config load, valid maximum is accepted, task listing keeps newest-first ordering, maintenance prunes due terminal tasks, fast-skip avoids expensive work on active records, and configured retention is honored instead of the default.
- **What was not tested**: Nothing else for this PR's changed behavior beyond the isolated local runtime/CLI proof and supplemental automated validation.
